title: salix lanata subsp. calcicola
intro: Salix lanata subsp. calcicola, also known as the Alpine willow, is a subspecies of the Salix lanata plant, which is a type of willow tree native to high-altitude mountainous regions in Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of salicin and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Salix lanata subsp. calcicola include reducing pain and inflammation, treating wounds and skin conditions, and providing relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Salix lanata subsp. calcicola can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it can also be used as a poultice or ointment to apply directly to the affected area.
